# CI/CD provider settings checklist

These are the settings a human must apply in the GitHub UI. The pipeline files
cannot set them themselves. Provider: **GitHub Actions**. Main branch: **main**.

## Branch protection for `main`

GitHub → repo Settings → Branches → Add branch ruleset (or classic protection)
for `main`:

- [ ] Require status checks to pass before merging, and require the **`verify`**
      workflow (job `byteswarm`) as a required check.
- [ ] Require at least **1** approving review before merge (enforces the
      no-self-merge constraint).
- [ ] Disallow **force-push** to `main`.
- [ ] (Recommended) Require branches to be up to date before merging.

## Secrets

The generated pipelines (`verify.yml`, `package.yml`) reference **no secrets**.
Nothing to add yet.

When a `deploy.yml` is later generated (see note below), it will need
**environment-scoped** secrets under GitHub → Settings → Environments (one
environment per deploy target: vm / container / kubernetes). Reference them by
name only (`${{ secrets.NAME }}`) — never echo them or pass them as
command-line arguments.

## Notes

- **`deploy.yml` was not generated.** The deployment ADR (0006) defines the
  posture and targets (static Go binary + container image → VM / container /
  Kubernetes), but no stack defines a `deploy` CI step command, so there is no
  verbatim command to place in the pipeline. Generate `deploy.yml` once a
  concrete per-target deploy command / thin adapter exists.
- **Green definition** for the bootstrap `ci_green` exit criterion: the
  `verify` workflow has at least one completed run with conclusion `success` on
  the bootstrap branch/PR (`gh run list --workflow verify --json conclusion`).
